name: HumanLayer
description: Open-source IDE (CodeLayer) that orchestrates AI coding agents with battle-tested workflows for parallel sessions and context engineering.

description.value: Open-source desktop IDE (CodeLayer) plus daemon (hld) and MCP CLI (hlyr) orchestrating parallel Claude Code sessions with approval gates, worktrees, and the 'Advanced Context Engineering' research/plan/implement workflow.

creator.value: HumanLayer (founded by Dexter Horthy; Y Combinator F24)
creator.comment: Company founded 2023, San Francisco; publicly launched as YC F24 company via HN Launch in Nov 2024.

first-release.comment: HumanLayer SDK / platform publicly launched via YC Launch HN in November 2024. CodeLayer IDE emerged later as the coding-agent-focused IDE.

latest-release.comment: As of 2026-04-23: only nightly builds published (e.g. codelayer-0.1.0-nightly-20260227142452, Feb 27, 2026). CodeLayer has not yet cut a stable (non-nightly) release.

license.comment: HumanLayer SDK and CodeLayer sources both Apache 2.0.

pricing-model.comment: CodeLayer IDE and hlyr CLI are free/OSS (Apache 2.0). The optional HumanLayer Cloud API for approval delivery has a freemium model (free Starter tier up to 1000 operations/mo, Premium at $500/mo, enterprise VPC deployment).

covered-phases.comment: ACE methodology prescribes a three-phase workflow: Research (understand the codebase → maps to discovery), Plan (precise implementation steps), Implement (execute phase-by-phase). Human review gates sit before each phase but are not themselves a distinct artifact phase.

methodology-style.comment: Core contribution is the Research→Plan→Implement loop ('Frequent Intentional Compaction') plus the CodeLayer desktop harness that runs Claude Code sessions in parallel. Not spec-centric in the SpecKit/OpenSpec sense.

opinionated.comment: ACE methodology is strongly opinionated about phase ordering and context-window management (target 40-60% utilization). CodeLayer harness is keyboard-first with prescribed session lifecycle.

ceremony-level.comment: Markdown research and plan artifacts with human review gates at each, but fewer distinct artifact kinds and review gates than BMAD or SpecKit.

mechanization-level.comment: CodeLayer is a full harness: Go daemon (hld) + Tauri/React desktop UI + TypeScript MCP CLI, orchestrating Claude Code sessions with SQLite persistence, session forking, and auto-accept controls.

hitl-checkpoints.comment: Human-in-the-loop is the company's founding thesis. CodeLayer MCP server injects deterministic approval gates; ACE methodology centers human review on research and plan artifacts.

approval-gates.comment: ACE deliberately places human review on the highest-leverage artifacts (research ≈ spec, plan) rather than on individual code edits. Implementation-phase tool calls can be approved per-tool via MCP or auto-accepted.

autonomous-e2e.comment: CodeLayer session settings expose 'Auto-Accept Edits' and selective tool auto-approval, allowing end-to-end execution without interactive human approval when configured.

autonomy-level.comment: Default configuration emphasizes phase-level human approvals at research and plan gates; per-tool auto-accept exists but is opt-in.

rollback-support.comment: Session forking lets users branch from any point in a session's history; worktree-based parallel sessions isolate changes so unwanted work can be discarded at the git level.

supported-agents.comment: CodeLayer is explicitly 'built on Claude Code' and positioned as 'Superhuman for Claude Code'. The separate HumanLayer SDK (for approvals) is model-agnostic, but the coding-agent workflow is currently Claude Code only.

editor-integrations.comment: CodeLayer is a standalone Tauri/React desktop IDE, not a plugin to an existing editor. None of the defined tags (vscode, jetbrains, neovim, terminal-only, web) fit cleanly.

model-agnostic.comment: CodeLayer workflow is built on and tightly coupled to Claude Code (Anthropic models). The HumanLayer SDK itself is provider-agnostic, but that is a separate product layer.

mcp-support.comment: hlyr implements an MCP server (the 'codelayer' server) that Claude Code connects to for approval gates and tool access.

artifact-formats.comment: Research and plan artifacts are produced as structured markdown files committed alongside source code.

artifacts-covered.comment: ACE produces two primary artifact kinds: research documents (spec-like understanding of the problem) and plans (precise implementation steps). No separate tasks/ADR/release-notes artifacts in the core methodology.

project-layout.value: CodeLayer desktop app manages session state in its own local SQLite store (via the hld daemon); per-project workflow artifacts are plain markdown files (research docs, plans, CLAUDE.md) stored wherever the user chooses within the project repo — there is no enforced directory convention like .speckit or .bmad.

artifacts-in-git.comment: Markdown research and plan artifacts live in the project repo and are tracked in git. Session conversation history lives in CodeLayer's local SQLite store, not git.

language-agnostic.comment: Case studies cite applying the methodology to a 300k-LOC Rust codebase and shipping 35k LOC of WASM/cancellation work, demonstrating cross-language applicability.

enforcement-mechanisms.comment: Phase discipline is enforced primarily through prompt instructions and the harness's deterministic MCP approval gates (manual-approval). No linters or test-gates are built into the methodology.

strict-phase-ordering.comment: ACE explicitly prescribes Research → Plan → Implement; the author argues errors in earlier phases multiply downstream, so the ordering is load-bearing.

artifact-validation.comment: Research and plan markdown is free-form; no schema validation or linting of artifacts is part of the workflow.

memory-implementation.comment: Per-project memory: CLAUDE.md and other markdown context files in the repo. Per-session memory: SQLite store managed by the hld daemon persisting full conversation histories and tool-call traces.

persistent-memory.comment: hld daemon persists full session state (messages, tool calls, results) to SQLite and streams updates via SSE; sessions can be resumed or forked across runs.

project-context-files.comment: HumanLayer's own repo ships a CLAUDE.md; project-scoped context files are a first-class part of the Claude Code workflow CodeLayer builds on.

cost-tracking.comment: No first-class token/cost tracking feature is documented in the README, DeepWiki, or ACE doc as of 2026-04-23. The ACE author anecdotally reports ~$12k/mo team Claude Opus spend, suggesting external tracking.

run-history.comment: hld daemon stores complete session histories (messages, tool calls, results) in SQLite; CodeLayer UI provides session table, fuzzy search, and detail views.

telemetry-opt.comment: Telemetry posture not clearly documented in public README or docs as of 2026-04-23. The project is self-hosted OSS; telemetry policy for the CodeLayer binary is not stated on the landing page or top-level README.

custom-templates.comment: Not explicitly documented. ACE methodology uses custom prompts/subagents for the Research phase, implying user-provided templates are expected, but no formal template system is described in public docs.

hooks.comment: Deterministic MCP approval gates function as hook points, and session settings expose auto-accept toggles, but there is no documented user-scriptable lifecycle-hook mechanism (pre-phase, post-phase) in the public docs as of 2026-04-23.

sub-agents.comment: ACE methodology explicitly uses 'dedicated subagents or custom prompts' for the Research phase, leveraging Claude Code's subagent capability.

marketplace.comment: No public marketplace or shared-template registry for HumanLayer/CodeLayer as of 2026-04-23.

install-mechanism.comment: hlyr CLI installable via npx (e.g. `npx humanlayer ...`); CodeLayer desktop app distributed as a macOS binary via GitHub Releases (nightly builds as of 2026-04-23).

setup-time.comment: Install nightly CodeLayer binary, configure Claude Code MCP connection, optionally join waitlist. Not a one-command setup.

self-hosted.comment: CodeLayer IDE + hld daemon + hlyr CLI all run locally on the user's machine. HumanLayer Cloud API (for off-device approval delivery via Slack/SMS/email) is an optional hosted service.

requires-account.comment: Core OSS CodeLayer workflow runs without a HumanLayer account; an account is only required for the optional cloud approval API. Landing page has a waitlist signup but it gates early-access marketing rather than the OSS binaries.

git-worktree-support.comment: Worktrees are an explicit, first-class feature: CodeLayer runs parallel Claude Code sessions in isolated git worktrees ('Worktrees? Done.').

documentation-quality.comment: Top-level README is thin ('Coming soon' install, waitlist-heavy). Depth exists but scattered: CLAUDE.md, DEVELOPMENT.md, the separate ace-fca.md methodology doc, and DeepWiki generated docs. Adequate for determined users, not polished.

active-maintenance.comment: Nightly releases published daily through at least 2026-02-27; 2,097+ commits. Clearly within 90-day active threshold.

example-projects.comment: ACE doc cites concrete case studies: 300k-LOC Rust codebase bug fix, 35k LOC shipped in 7 hours. YC launch talk 'Advanced Context Engineering for Coding Agents' (Aug 2025) provides walkthrough.

community-channel.comment: Official Discord at humanlayer.dev/discord (redirects to discord.gg/3chc2y8jpT).
